stage.name: EX_STAGE map: where backend work actually runs
stage.description: A source-grounded map of CVA6 execution units, result return, side-effect buffering, and the issue-to-commit boundary.
stage.courseTemplateName: Professional Technical Deep Dive - Evidence-Grounded SlideKit

scenes.title: The execute stage is a small city, not one box

content.title: EX_STAGE map: where backend work actually runs
content.subtitle: After issue, instructions enter a cluster of units with different latency, side effects, and return paths.
content.thesis: Do not read execute as one monolithic step. Read it as a set of functional units whose results must return cleanly and whose side effects must wait for commit.

sourceAnchors.title: CV32A60X design: EX_STAGE module
sourceAnchors.claimSupported: EX_STAGE is the logical execute stage and encapsulates ALU, Branch Unit, CSR buffer, Mult, load/store, and CVXIF.

content.speakerNotes: Unit 06 begins at the place where issued work actually runs. The official C V A six design text says EX_STAGE encapsulates ALU, Branch Unit, CSR buffer, Mult, load and store, and CVXIF. That list is not cosmetic. Each unit has a different timing story, a different side-effect story, and a different way of reporting back to the rest of the backend.

actions.text: Unit 06 begins at the place where issued work actually runs. The official C V A six design text says EX_STAGE encapsulates ALU, Branch Unit, CSR buffer, Mult, load and store, and CVXIF. That list is not cosmetic. Each unit has a different timing story, a different side-effect story, and a different way of reporting back to the rest of the backend.

scenes.title: Place EX_STAGE in the full machine

content.title: EX_STAGE sits between disciplined issue and disciplined commit
content.body: The useful mental path is ISSUE_STAGE sends a tracked operation, EX_STAGE performs or buffers work, ISSUE_STAGE receives completed results, and COMMIT_STAGE makes architectural state real.
content.caption: Use the overview diagram as a map; this lesson zooms into the execute box.

sourceAnchors.title: CV32A60X design: EX_STAGE module
sourceAnchors.claimSupported: EX_STAGE is the logical execute stage and encapsulates ALU, Branch Unit, CSR buffer, Mult, load/store, and CVXIF.

sourceAnchors.title: CV32A60X design: COMMIT_STAGE module
sourceAnchors.claimSupported: COMMIT_STAGE is the last pipeline stage and updates architectural state by writing CSR registers, committing stores, and writing back data to the register file.

content.speakerNotes: Here is the first boundary to keep clean. Issue decides whether work may enter the backend and keeps the scoreboard story. Execute performs the operation or holds the side-effect information. Commit is the last authority for architectural state. If a lesson mixes those three jobs, students may get a pretty diagram but a fuzzy processor. We want the opposite.

actions.text: Here is the first boundary to keep clean. Issue decides whether work may enter the backend and keeps the scoreboard story. Execute performs the operation or holds the side-effect information. Commit is the last authority for architectural state. If a lesson mixes those three jobs, students may get a pretty diagram but a fuzzy processor. We want the opposite.

content.title: Five common EX_STAGE contracts to recognize
content.subtitle: The same stage name hides several very different engineering contracts.

cards.title: ALU
cards.body: Single-cycle arithmetic and bitwise work; result returns directly to ISSUE_STAGE.

cards.title: Branch
cards.body: Checks prediction reality, calculates target, and reports corrective actions.

cards.title: CSR buffer
cards.body: Holds CSR operation information until commit decides whether side effects are allowed.

cards.title: Mult / Div
cards.body: Multiplication is fixed and pipelined; division is much longer and variable.

cards.title: LSU
cards.body: Coordinates load result return, store buffering, memory access, and store commit.

content.takeaway: A professional explanation names the unit and its contract before naming the signal.

sourceAnchors.title: CV32A60X design: ALU submodule
sourceAnchors.claimSupported: The ALU performs 32-bit or 64-bit arithmetic and bitwise operations, completes in a single cycle, and returns result_o to ISSUE_STAGE.

sourceAnchors.title: CV32A60X design: Branch Unit submodule
sourceAnchors.claimSupported: The Branch Unit manages conditional and unconditional jumps, calculates targets, decides take or not take, and reports corrective actions.

sourceAnchors.title: CV32A60X design: CSR buffer submodule
sourceAnchors.claimSupported: The CSR buffer stores CSR address and operation information until COMMIT_STAGE decides whether the CSR side effect should execute.

sourceAnchors.title: CV32A60X design: Mult submodule
sourceAnchors.claimSupported: The multiplier is pipelined with a fixed two-cycle multiplication latency, while serial division has much longer variable latency.

sourceAnchors.title: CV32A60X design: Load Store Unit
sourceAnchors.claimSupported: The LSU handles load and store requests, store commit interaction, load result return, and selected ordering checks with the store buffer.

content.speakerNotes: The technical move is to avoid saying every instruction simply executes. An ALU operation can finish in one cycle. A branch must compare prediction with reality. A CSR instruction may carry a side effect that cannot happen speculatively. A multiply and a divide are not the same timing problem. A load or store is tied to memory ordering and store commit. One stage, several contracts.

actions.text: The technical move is to avoid saying every instruction simply executes. An ALU operation can finish in one cycle. A branch must compare prediction with reality. A CSR instruction may carry a side effect that cannot happen speculatively. A multiply and a divide are not the same timing problem. A load or store is tied to memory ordering and store commit. One stage, several contracts.

scenes.title: The result path is part of execution

content.title: Work does not end when the unit computes a value
content.subtitle: A result still needs identity, readiness, and a safe architectural landing.

steps.title: Issued entry arrives
steps.body: The issued operation carries scoreboard identity into EX_STAGE.

steps.title: Unit performs work
steps.body: The selected functional unit computes, waits, or buffers side-effect information.

steps.title: Result returns
steps.body: Result plus transaction identity goes back so ISSUE_STAGE can update tracked state.

steps.title: Commit decides
steps.body: Only completed, oldest work may update architectural state.

content.takeaway: Execute produces evidence; commit turns the right evidence into state.

sourceAnchors.title: CV32A60X design: EX_STAGE module
sourceAnchors.claimSupported: EX_STAGE is the logical execute stage and encapsulates ALU, Branch Unit, CSR buffer, Mult, load/store, and CVXIF.

sourceAnchors.title: CVA6 deprecated design docs: EX stage
sourceAnchors.claimSupported: The legacy EX stage design note explains valid/ready FU handshakes, transaction IDs, branch corrective action, and CSR-buffer commit discipline.

sourceAnchors.title: CV32A60X design: COMMIT_STAGE module
sourceAnchors.claimSupported: COMMIT_STAGE is the last pipeline stage and updates architectural state by writing CSR registers, committing stores, and writing back data to the register file.

content.speakerNotes: A good backend reader watches identity as much as data. The operation enters execute with a scoreboard identity. The selected unit may finish immediately or later. When a result returns, the backend must know which instruction it belongs to. Then commit decides whether that completed work is allowed to change architectural state. That is the thread tying Unit 05 to Unit 06.

actions.text: A good backend reader watches identity as much as data. The operation enters execute with a scoreboard identity. The selected unit may finish immediately or later. When a result returns, the backend must know which instruction it belongs to. Then commit decides whether that completed work is allowed to change architectural state. That is the thread tying Unit 05 to Unit 06.

scenes.title: Three execution categories keep the map readable

content.title: Classify each unit by timing and side effects
content.subtitle: This simple classification prevents shallow pipeline explanations.

columns.title: Value-producing work
columns.body: ALU and many branch decisions produce a result or control decision that can be reported with identity.

columns.title: State-changing work
columns.body: CSR writes and stores require extra caution because a wrong-path side effect would corrupt architectural state.

content.takeaway: Execution may compute early, but state changes must be disciplined.

sourceAnchors.title: CV32A60X design: ALU submodule
sourceAnchors.claimSupported: The ALU performs 32-bit or 64-bit arithmetic and bitwise operations, completes in a single cycle, and returns result_o to ISSUE_STAGE.

sourceAnchors.title: CV32A60X design: CSR buffer submodule
sourceAnchors.claimSupported: The CSR buffer stores CSR address and operation information until COMMIT_STAGE decides whether the CSR side effect should execute.

sourceAnchors.title: CV32A60X design: Load Store Unit
sourceAnchors.claimSupported: The LSU handles load and store requests, store commit interaction, load result return, and selected ordering checks with the store buffer.

sourceAnchors.title: CVA6 deprecated design docs: Commit stage
sourceAnchors.claimSupported: The legacy commit-stage note states the design rule that no other pipeline stage updates architectural state under any circumstances.

content.speakerNotes: This is the core reading method for the unit. First ask whether the operation mainly produces a value, mainly redirects control, or potentially changes architectural state. Value production is about result and identity. Control is about prediction correction. State changes are about discipline: CSR writes and stores cannot be casually applied just because a unit saw the instruction. Commit has to arbitrate.

actions.text: This is the core reading method for the unit. First ask whether the operation mainly produces a value, mainly redirects control, or potentially changes architectural state. Value production is about result and identity. Control is about prediction correction. State changes are about discipline: CSR writes and stores cannot be casually applied just because a unit saw the instruction. Commit has to arbitrate.

questions.question: Which statement is the safest description of EX_STAGE in this lesson?

options.label: It is one ALU-like box where all instructions finish the same way
It is a logical stage containing several functional units with different contracts
It is responsible for final architectural retirement

questions.analysis: The source lists several EX_STAGE submodules; their timing and side-effect behavior differ.

questions.question: Which items are part of the EX_STAGE unit map described by the CV32A60X design text?
questions.analysis: The EX_STAGE description names ALU, Branch Unit, CSR buffer, Mult, load/store, and CVXIF.

questions.question: Why should CSR and store behavior be treated carefully?

options.label: They are always disabled in CV32A60X
They can affect architectural state and therefore need commit discipline
They never interact with the backend

questions.analysis: State-changing behavior must wait for the commit decision to avoid wrong-path side effects.

questions.question: What is the best bridge from Unit 05 to Unit 06?

options.label: The scoreboard identity lets returned execute results be matched to the right instruction
Commit can ignore execution result identity
Branch prediction removes all backend control work

questions.analysis: The backend needs identity plus result readiness before commit can retire the right instruction.

sourceAnchors.title: CV32A60X design: EX_STAGE module
sourceAnchors.claimSupported: EX_STAGE is the logical execute stage and encapsulates ALU, Branch Unit, CSR buffer, Mult, load/store, and CVXIF.

sourceAnchors.title: CV32A60X design: COMMIT_STAGE module
sourceAnchors.claimSupported: COMMIT_STAGE is the last pipeline stage and updates architectural state by writing CSR registers, committing stores, and writing back data to the register file.

content.speakerNotes: This quiz checks the map, not memorization. You should be able to say that EX_STAGE is a cluster of units, that CSR and store behavior require commit discipline, and that result identity links execute back to the scoreboard. If those three ideas are stable, the next lessons can go deeper without turning into signal soup.

actions.text: This quiz checks the map, not memorization. You should be able to say that EX_STAGE is a cluster of units, that CSR and store behavior require commit discipline, and that result identity links execute back to the scoreboard. If those three ideas are stable, the next lessons can go deeper without turning into signal soup.

scenes.title: You can now read execute as contracts

content.title: EX_STAGE is a contract map
takeaways: ALU, Branch, CSR buffer, Mult, LSU, and CVXIF should not be explained as identical work.
Execution results need identity and readiness before they can become architectural state.
CSR and store side effects are intentionally tied to commit discipline.
content.nextStep: Next lesson: Branch Unit, where frontend prediction is checked against execute-stage reality.
content.closing: The backend becomes readable when every unit has a contract.

sourceAnchors.title: CV32A60X design: EX_STAGE module
sourceAnchors.claimSupported: EX_STAGE is the logical execute stage and encapsulates ALU, Branch Unit, CSR buffer, Mult, load/store, and CVXIF.

content.speakerNotes: The takeaway is not a long list of submodules. The takeaway is a contract map. ALU, Branch, CSR buffer, Mult, and LSU each answer a different question. Results have to return with identity. Side effects need commit discipline. Next we zoom into one of the most important contracts: the Branch Unit, where prediction meets reality.

actions.text: The takeaway is not a long list of submodules. The takeaway is a contract map. ALU, Branch, CSR buffer, Mult, and LSU each answer a different question. Results have to return with identity. Side effects need commit discipline. Next we zoom into one of the most important contracts: the Branch Unit, where prediction meets reality.
